_meta.description: Biomarker prevalence in TCGA-GBM cohort (n=596 cases with genomic data)
_meta.source: TCGA GBM publications (Nature 2008, Cell 2013) and cBioPortal TCGA-GBM dataset
_meta.note: Counts are approximate and based on published TCGA-GBM analyses. Some markers have data on fewer patients due to assay coverage.

MGMT promoter methylated.note: ~45% of GBM show MGMT promoter methylation (associated with better temozolomide response)

MGMT methylated.note: ~45% of GBM show MGMT promoter methylation

MGMT unmethylated.note: ~55% of GBM have unmethylated MGMT promoter

MGMT status known.note: MGMT methylation status available for most TCGA-GBM cases (~45% methylated, ~55% unmethylated)

MGMT mentioned.note: MGMT is referenced but specific methylation status not determined from criteria

IDH1 R132H mutation.note: ~5% of GBM carry IDH1 R132H; most are secondary GBM

IDH1 mutation.note: ~6% of GBM carry IDH1 mutations (R132H, R132C, etc.)

IDH mutation.note: ~6-7% of GBM carry IDH1 or IDH2 mutations

IDH wild-type.note: ~93% of GBM are IDH wild-type (primary GBM)

EGFRvIII.note: ~25% of GBM express EGFRvIII (constitutively active truncated variant)

EGFR amplification.note: ~44% of GBM show EGFR gene amplification

EGFR mutation.note: ~28% of GBM have EGFR mutations (including vIII and point mutations)

EGFR overexpression.note: ~60% of GBM show EGFR overexpression at protein level

EGFR alteration.note: ~57% of GBM have some EGFR alteration (mutation, amplification, or overexpression)

PTEN loss.note: ~36% of GBM show PTEN loss (deletion or reduced expression)

BRAF V600E mutation.note: ~1% of adult GBM carry BRAF V600E (more common in pediatric gliomas)

BRAF mutation.note: ~2% of GBM carry any BRAF mutation

TERT promoter mutation.note: ~80-83% of GBM carry TERT promoter mutations (C228T or C250T)

1p/19q codeletion.note: ~1% of GBM show 1p/19q codeletion (characteristic of oligodendroglioma, rare in GBM)

1p/19q.note: 1p/19q codeletion is rare in GBM (~1%), common in oligodendroglioma

ATRX loss.note: ~7% of GBM show ATRX loss (associated with IDH-mutant and ALT phenotype)

PDGFRA amplification.note: ~13% of GBM show PDGFRA amplification (proneural subtype)

CDKN2A deletion.note: ~58% of GBM show CDKN2A homozygous deletion

FGFR alteration.note: ~3% of GBM have FGFR alterations (mutations, amplifications, or fusions)

MET amplification.note: ~4% of primary GBM show MET amplification (higher in recurrent)

NF1 mutation.note: ~10% of GBM carry NF1 mutations/loss (mesenchymal subtype)

High TMB.note: ~5% of GBM are TMB-high (>10 mut/Mb); higher in recurrent post-TMZ

PD-L1 expression.note: ~37% of GBM show PD-L1 expression (variable by assay and cutoff)

Ki-67 expression.note: Ki-67 is universally expressed in GBM (high proliferation index, typically >10%)

H3K27M mutation.note: <1% of adult GBM; characteristic of diffuse midline glioma
